title: LIFECYCLE — DFU Blocked After App Backgrounding During Active Softphone Call (Voyager 5200)
steps: Precondition: Voyager 5200 connected to phone via Bluetooth. Poly Studio Mobile app installed. Zoom (or any softphone) configured.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app. Navigate to: Device → Software Update. Verify page shows 'No update available' or update prompt.
2. Press Home button to background the app.
3. Open Zoom (softphone). Place an active call (e.g., join a test meeting or call echo test number).
4. While call is active, return to Poly Studio Mobile app (foreground from recent apps).
5. Navigate to: Device → Software Update.
6. If an update is available, tap 'Update' / 'Install'.
7. Observe system behavior.
expected: Expected: Software Update page detects active call state and displays 'Cannot update during a call' message (or equivalent). The 'Update' button is disabled/greyed out. DFU firmware update is BLOCKED. Actual (If Bug): App fails to re-evaluate call state after being foregrounded. The update proceeds or the button is enabled, allowing DFU to start during the active softphone call — regression boundary: call-state awareness lost after app backgrounding + foregrounding cycle.

title: STATE TRIGGER — DFU Blocked When Navigating to Software Update After Call Already Active (Voyager 5200)
steps: Precondition: Voyager 5200 connected. Softphone (Zoom) installed and signed in.
1. Open Zoom (softphone). Place an active call.
2. While call is active, open Poly Studio Mobile app (fresh launch or from background).
3. Navigate: Device → Software Update (i.e., traverse Home → Device Settings → Software Update while call is already active).
4. If an update is available, attempt to tap 'Update'.
5. Observe the UI state of the Update button and any warning messages.
expected: Expected: App detects active call state upon entering the Software Update page (lazy check on page entry). Update button is disabled. Warning message displayed: 'Cannot update during a call'. DFU is blocked. Actual (If Bug): The call-state check only fires on initial app launch or page mount at app start. Navigating to Software Update after the call has already started bypasses the check — the Update button remains enabled, allowing DFU to proceed during the active call. Regression boundary: call-state check is not continuously monitored; it is evaluated only at page entry time.

title: DEVICE ASYMMETRY — DFU Blocked During Active Call on Voyager 5200 (Mono Headset Specific)
steps: Precondition: Voyager 5200 (mono headset) connected. No other Poly device connected.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app. Navigate: Device → Software Update.
2. Verify current firmware version displayed.
3. Place an active call using the native phone dialer (not softphone).
4. While call is active, navigate to: Device → Software Update.
5. If an update is available, attempt to initiate DFU.
6. Observe the result.
expected: Expected: DFU firmware update is BLOCKED. The app displays a warning that a call is in progress. The Update button is disabled. This validates Voyager 5200-specific DFU blocking logic (mono headset with single audio path — updating during a call would disrupt the only audio channel). Actual (If Bug): The DFU blocking logic works differently on mono headsets vs. TWS earbuds (e.g., Voyager Free 60+). On Voyager 5200, the update proceeds or the call-state check is absent — regression boundary: device asymmetry where mono headset DFU blocking is not implemented or uses a different code path than TWS devices.

title: SOFTPHONE SPECIFIC — DFU Not Blocked During Zoom Call (VoIP Call State Gap)
steps: Precondition: Voyager 5200 connected. Zoom (or other softphone) installed and configured with active account.
1. Open Zoom. Start/join a meeting or place a VoIP call (audio only).
2. While the Zoom call is active, open Poly Studio Mobile app.
3. Navigate: Device → Software Update.
4. If an update is available, attempt to tap 'Update'.
5. Observe whether the app detects the softphone call state.
6. Repeat steps 1-5 using a different softphone (e.g., Microsoft Teams, Skype) if available.
expected: Expected: DFU firmware update is BLOCKED. The app detects the softphone VoIP call state (via AudioManager or NotificationListener) and displays 'Cannot update during a call'. Update button disabled. Actual (If Bug): The app only checks TelephonyManager.CallState (native dialer calls) and does NOT detect softphone/VoIP call states. The Update button remains enabled and DFU proceeds during the Zoom call — regression boundary: softphone calls are invisible to the app's call-state detection, allowing DFU to corrupt the headset firmware during an active VoIP session.

title: IMMEDIATE STATE TRIGGER — DFU Blocked When Call Starts Mid-Update Page Visit (Voyager 5200)
steps: Precondition: Voyager 5200 connected. Softphone (Zoom) ready.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app. Navigate: Device → Software Update.
2. Verify the Software Update page is displayed and the Update button is enabled (no call active).
3. WITHOUT leaving the Software Update page, use another device or split-screen to place a Zoom call.
4. Return focus to the Poly Studio Mobile app (still on Software Update page).
5. Observe the Update button state and any UI changes.
6. If the button remains enabled, tap it to attempt DFU.
expected: Expected: The app continuously monitors call state. When the call starts, the Update button immediately disables and a warning appears ('Call in progress — update blocked'). DFU is blocked even though the user was already on the Software Update page before the call began. Actual (If Bug): The call-state check is performed only once on page entry. A call that starts while the user is already on the Software Update page is not detected. The Update button remains enabled, allowing DFU to start during the call — regression boundary: no real-time call-state listener; stale page-entry check only.
